MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth re-accelerated to +92% YoY with a $75.2B quarter; Q2 guided $91B as China DC compute goes to zero in the outlook.

shifts.trend: DC +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year) → +92% (Q1 FY27); Q2 guided to $91B (+12% QoQ)
shifts.note: Blackwell full-ramp quarter; every geography except China accelerating

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 73.5% (FY26 guide) → 74.9% (Q1 FY27 actual) → 74.9% guided Q2 FY27
shifts.note: Stable at high level despite full-system Blackwell mix; supply constraint not hurting GMs

shifts.trend: China DC compute: $3.0B Q3 FY26 → H20 effectively zero FY26 → explicitly excluded from Q2 FY27 guide
shifts.note: China optionality now fully impaired; model at zero for DC compute going forward

shifts.trend: Dividend $0.01/qtr (FY26) → $0.25/qtr (Q1 FY27, +25x); $80B new buyback; ~$20B returned in Q1 alone
shifts.note: Largest single-quarter capital return in company history; signals mgmt confidence in sustainable FCF

shifts.trend: FY26 10-K: 4 customers each >10% (22/15/13/11%); Q1 FY27 10-Q: concentration maintained but not freshly disclosed
shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence structural — no sign of broadening to enterprise/sovereign yet at scale

shifts.trend: Both filings cite data center energy, capital, and infrastructure availability as gating constraint on customer deployments
shifts.note: No easing language added; Blackwell 'sell-out' across all form factors per CEO commentary

NVDA.verdict: Beat-and-raise cadence intact and accelerating. Q1 FY27 DC $75.2B (+92% YoY) is the largest single-quarter DC revenue in company history. Q2 guide of $91B is deliberate sandbagging. China DC compute is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero. Supply and energy remain the gating constraints — the thesis is fully intact and the only incremental risk is the 4-way hyperscaler concentration formally disclosed in the FY26 10-K.

shifts.trend: Latest filing introduces tightness language absent from prior
shifts.note: auto-derived (new tightness — single-quarter, lower confidence)

shifts.trend: Supply-tightness language present in both latest filings
shifts.note: auto-derived from filing keyword presence

HPE.headline: +40% YoY with Networking up 148% on first full Juniper quarter; AI server backlog $5.9B entering Q3 with FY26 growth guidance raised to 29-33%.

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 +16% YoY → Q2 FY26 +40% YoY (first full Juniper quarter); AI orders doubled YoY in Q2
shifts.note: Juniper consolidation inflects reported revenue; underlying AI Systems demand is the organic driver

shifts.trend: Networking $2.69B (+148% YoY on Juniper); Server $5.45B (+32.7%); Cloud & AI total $7.71B
shifts.note: AI Systems backlog entering Q3: $5.9B — provides strong multi-quarter revenue visibility

shifts.trend: GAAP GM 36.5% (+810bps YoY); Juniper software/services mix accretive; AI system pricing premium maintained
shifts.note: Margin expansion faster than expected — Juniper integration synergies materializing

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue growth raised 29-33%; Q3 guided $11.5-12.1B; FY26 non-GAAP EPS $3.35-$3.45; FCF ≥$3.5B
shifts.note: Second consecutive FY26 raise; Q3 midpoint $11.8B would be largest quarter in HPE history

HPE.verdict: HPE's Juniper acquisition is delivering faster than Street expected. Networking +148% and total revenue +40% validate the portfolio strategy. The $5.9B AI backlog entering Q3 provides visibility through FY26. The margin expansion story (+810bps GAAP GM) is the positive surprise. FY26 guidance raised for the second consecutive quarter.

CSCO.headline: AI networking pivot accelerating — $5.3B AI orders YTD with FY26 AI revenue guidance raised $1B to $4B; Q4 guided to record $16.7-16.9B.

shifts.trend: Revenue YoY: Q2 FY26 +9% → Q3 FY26 +12%; Networking product +25% YoY in Q3
shifts.note: AI networking orders pulling total revenue acceleration; Q4 guided to record

shifts.trend: FY26 AI revenue target: $3B → $4B raised; AI orders YTD $5.3B with one quarter remaining
shifts.note: AI order pace implies FY26 AI orders >$6B; $4B revenue target may prove conservative

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: stable ~66-67% but GAAP GM down ~3pts on hardware mix (Splunk + networking hardware increase)
shifts.note: Transient — hardware mix shift normalizes as Splunk integrates; long-term GM trajectory guided flat

shifts.trend: FY26 full-year raised; Q4 guided $16.7-16.9B revenue (record quarter)
shifts.note: Implies full-year $62.8-63.0B; largest annual revenue in CSCO history

CSCO.verdict: CSCO's AI rotation is ahead of schedule. YTD AI orders of $5.3B with one quarter remaining already exceed the original $3B FY26 revenue target. Networking's +25% growth is the strongest in a decade. The 4,000-person workforce reorg redirects savings into AI R&D without margin compression. Margin pressure from hardware mix is transient.

AMAT.headline: Record $7.91B quarter with 25-year-high gross margins; WFE CY2026 outlook raised to >30% as advanced packaging exceeds 50% of AMAT revenue mix.

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 $7.17B → Q2 FY26 record $7.91B (+10% YoY); Q3 guided $8.95B (+13% QoQ)
shifts.note: Sequential acceleration; WFE CY2026 >30% vs >20% prior guide drives multi-quarter tailwind

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM: Q1 49.7% → Q2 50.0% (25-year high); Q3 guided ~50.1%
shifts.note: Advanced packaging + HBM pricing power flowing through; AGS service segment margin accretive

shifts.trend: WFE CY2026: >20% → >30%; Q3 guided $8.95B (new record if achieved)
shifts.note: Third consecutive quarter of WFE guide raises; credibility high given execution track record

shifts.trend: Advanced packaging >50% of AMAT CY2026 revenue; DRAM +18% YoY mix share gain
shifts.note: HBM4/CoWoS capacity remains supply-constrained; AMAT positioned at the choke-point

AMAT.verdict: AMAT is at the center of the WFE supercycle. Raised WFE >30% guide reflects HBM/advanced-packaging demand pulling forward capacity investment faster than consensus expected. Gross margins at 25-year highs confirm pricing power. China stable at 24%. Advanced packaging majority of revenue is the structural shift; the Q3 $8.95B guide sets up a new record.

MRVL.headline: Record $2.418B quarter with DC custom ASIC+interconnect at 76% of revenue; $3B quarterly run rate pulled forward to Q3 FY27.

shifts.trend: FY26 revenue $6.9B → Q1 FY27 $2.418B (+28% YoY); Q2 guided $2.7B (+12% QoQ); $3B run rate Q3 FY27
shifts.note: $3B quarterly milestone pulled forward by one quarter — execution ahead of prior guide

shifts.trend: DC revenue: Q1 FY27 $1.833B (76% of total, +27% YoY); FY27 DC ~50% growth; interconnect >70%
shifts.note: Celestial AI + XConn acquisitions (closed Feb 2026) deepen optical interconnect; custom ASIC backlog multi-year

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 58.9% in Q1 FY27; prior FY26 ~58%; stable as mix shifts further toward DC
shifts.note: Custom ASIC mix at scale preserving margins; interconnect dilutive but offset by volume

shifts.trend: Q2 guided $2.7B; $3B run rate Q3 FY27 (vs prior Q4 FY27 target); FY27 DC growth raised to ~50%
shifts.note: Guide raise on an already-high base; forward visibility exceptional from multi-year customer commitments

MRVL.verdict: MRVL is the cleanest custom AI-ASIC and optical interconnect pure-play. DC at 76% of revenue growing >27% YoY with the $3B quarterly run rate arriving one quarter sooner than guided. Celestial AI and XConn add optical depth. FY27 DC ~50% growth guide implies $8B+ DC revenue for the year — the revenue inflection is structural, not cyclical.

SNPS.headline: +42% YoY on Ansys consolidation; Design IP recovering sequentially (+12% QoQ) with FY26 guidance raised to $9.67B midpoint and $11B backlog.

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 +42% YoY (first Ansys-consolidated quarter); Q2 FY26 +42% YoY; FY26 $9.665B midpoint
shifts.note: Inorganic Ansys contribution ~$3B annualized; organic EDA market also growing on chip complexity

shifts.trend: Design IP: -6% YoY (Q1) → -6% YoY but +12% QoQ (Q2) — sequential inflection underway
shifts.note: China BIS restrictions + macro caused IP softness; sequential recovery signals worst is behind

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP operating margin ~41% both quarters; EPS $3.35 (Q2) vs ~$3.15 (Q1); on track for FY26 $14.76
shifts.note: Integration costs absorbed without margin compression; Ansys synergies beginning to flow through

shifts.trend: FY26: $9.625-9.705B raised; FCF ~$2B; backlog ~$11B provides multi-year revenue floor
shifts.note: Second consecutive FY26 raise; $11B backlog is 1.1x FY26 revenue — exceptional visibility

shifts.trend: China Design IP: 'challenging near-term' on macro + BIS restrictions — same language as Q1
shifts.note: No easing signal; China BIS headwinds stable at a lower IP revenue level

SNPS.verdict: Synopsys is integrating Ansys ahead of schedule with no margin compression. Design IP sequential recovery (+12% QoQ) is the critical positive inflection. China BIS headwinds are contained at the IP segment level and appear to be stabilizing. $11B backlog at 1.1x FY26 revenue is exceptional visibility for a software business. FY26 guidance raised for the second consecutive quarter.

MOD.headline: FY26 record $3.18B (+23%) with Q4 single-quarter record $954M; $165M deposit + >$4B contracted DC cooling revenue signals the pivot to data center is complete.

shifts.trend: FY25 $2.59B → FY26 $3.18B (+23%); Q4 $954M (+47% YoY) — strongest quarterly revenue in company history
shifts.note: DC cooling becoming primary revenue driver as GPU power density forces liquid cooling adoption

shifts.trend: Data center = 35% of Climate Solutions revenue (FY26); expanding from <20% two years prior
shifts.note: Rapid mix shift; DC economics superior (higher ASPs, longer service contracts)

shifts.trend: $165M upfront deposit received; >$4B contracted revenue 2027-2029 from long-term capacity agreement
shifts.note: Customer advance payment signals pricing power and supply scarcity in liquid cooling capacity

shifts.trend: >$4B revenue pipeline 2027-2029 from contracted agreements; Performance Technologies RMT pending
shifts.note: Post-RMT entity will be a DC cooling pure-play — clearer valuation multiple expected

MOD.verdict: Modine has completed the pivot to data center cooling. The $165M advance payment and >$4B contracted pipeline (2027-2029) are the clearest evidence of supply scarcity in liquid cooling capacity. Q4's +47% YoY record quarter shows the DC transition compounding. The Performance Technologies RMT with Gentherm (pending end CY2026) will crystallize the DC cooling pure-play valuation story.

OKLO.headline: Aurora-INL groundbreaking + DOE PDSA submission mark key regulatory milestones; NVIDIA AI-nuclear partnership announced with $2.5B cash buffer.

shifts.trend: Q4 FY25: no plant groundbreaking → Q1 FY26: Aurora-INL groundbreaking + PDSA submitted to DOE
shifts.note: Regulatory milestones sequencing faster than consensus expected; NRC licensing still the long pole

shifts.trend: FY25 capex modest → Q1 FY26 capex $32.8M: Groves isotope test reactor completed; Idaho Radiochemistry Lab advancing
shifts.note: Deployment capex beginning — Groves completion in 229 days is a construction execution proof point

shifts.trend: Pre-revenue across both periods; Idaho Radiochemistry Lab first customer contracts possible 2026
shifts.note: NVIDIA partnership adds strategic credibility and potential anchor customer; Aurora commercial ops late 2020s

OKLO.verdict: OKLO is executing on the regulatory path. Aurora-INL groundbreaking and PDSA DOE submission are sequential milestones toward NRC licensing. The NVIDIA partnership adds strategic validation for AI-nuclear power demand. Pre-revenue with $2.5B cash (no near-term dilution risk). Key catalysts: NRC licensing progress, first Idaho Radiochemistry Lab contract, and any additional hyperscaler power partnership announcements.

CEG.headline: First full Calpine quarter: Q1 revenue $11.1B with ~$2/share accretion; nuclear 92.3% capacity factor + CyrusOne co-location approval advancing AI power thesis.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue ~$7.9B → Q1 FY26 $11.1B (Calpine first full quarter); adj EPS $2.74 (+28% YoY)
shifts.note: Calpine accretion ~$2/share full-year FY26; integration on track

shifts.trend: Nuclear: 44,666 GWh at 92.3% CF (industry-leading); Combined cycle 23M MWh (Calpine contribution)
shifts.note: Nuclear + gas together create baseload + peaking portfolio ideal for 24/7 AI data center power

shifts.trend: FY26 adj operating EPS $11-12 reaffirmed; 20% long-term EPS growth target maintained
shifts.note: No raise but no cut — management confident in the original post-Calpine financial model

shifts.trend: CyrusOne co-location at Freestone approved; first concrete AI power deal beyond PPA structures
shifts.note: Hyperscaler power partnerships diversifying CEG's revenue mix toward contracted long-duration agreements

CEG.verdict: CEG is delivering on the Calpine acquisition thesis ahead of schedule. Nuclear 92.3% capacity factor operationally strong. CyrusOne Freestone co-location is the first concrete AI data center power deal. FY26 EPS guidance $11-12 reaffirmed with 20% long-term growth target. The power-for-AI narrative continues to strengthen — CEG's combined nuclear + gas portfolio is uniquely positioned to serve 24/7 AI power demand.

SNOW.headline: Product revenue reaccelerates to +34% YoY (from +30%); NRR expands to 126%; FY27 guidance raised to $5.84B with $6B AWS commitment.

shifts.trend: FY26 product revenue $4.47B (+28% YoY) → Q1 FY27 $1.33B (+34% YoY, vs +30% Q4 FY26 rate)
shifts.note: Sequential re-acceleration after FY26 deceleration concern; Cortex AI workloads driving incremental consumption

shifts.trend: RPO $9.21B (+38% YoY); NRR 126% (up from 125%); net new customers +616 (healthy pace)
shifts.note: NRR expansion signals installed-base monetization cycle intact; RPO growth ahead of revenue growth

shifts.trend: FY27 product revenue raised to $5.84B (31% growth); Q2 guided $1.415-1.42B
shifts.note: Raised guidance + re-accelerating growth = rare combination in enterprise SaaS at this scale

shifts.trend: $6B 5-year AWS commitment deepens hyperscaler infrastructure dependency
shifts.note: Strategically aligned but concentrates cloud infrastructure spend with one vendor

SNOW.verdict: SNOW is re-entering a zone of accelerating revenue growth. Product revenue re-inflecting (+34% vs +30% prior quarter) and NRR expansion to 126% prove the installed-base monetization cycle is intact. The $6B AWS commitment provides infrastructure runway at scale but is a concentration consideration. AI monetization through Cortex/Data Cloud is the new growth vector on top of the core data platform.

CRM.headline: Agentforce AWUs +111% QoQ with $1.2B ARR growing 205% YoY; non-GAAP margins +250bps as AI agents emerge as a distinct high-growth business inside CRM.

shifts.note: Core platform re-accelerating modestly; Informatica adds ~4pts; Agentforce adds premium pricing

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP operating margin: FY26 ~33% → Q1 FY27 34.8% (+250bps YoY); EPS beat by 24%
shifts.note: Margin expansion while absorbing Agentforce + Informatica investment — execution discipline

shifts.trend: Agentforce ARR $1.2B (+205% YoY); AWUs 3.8B (+111% QoQ); Data Cloud+Informatica $1.1B ARR
shifts.note: Total AI-adjacent ARR ~$3.4B; AWU growth is the fastest new-product ramp in CRM history

shifts.trend: Q2 FY27: $11.27-11.35B (+10-11%); Informatica ~4pts of Q2 growth; full-year trajectory maintained
shifts.note: Conservative guidance culture intact; beat cadence suggests Q2 actuals likely above guide

CRM.verdict: CRM is successfully monetizing the Agentforce agent layer. AWU growth (+111% QoQ) and ARR ($1.2B, +205% YoY) prove early product-market fit for enterprise AI agents. Non-GAAP margins continuing to expand (+250bps) while reinvesting in AI demonstrate execution discipline. Informatica adds data infrastructure depth. The core CRM SaaS platform is maturing while Agentforce becomes the new high-velocity growth vector.
